notes: [logic/values/timing] race.mode.setup_limits: Unset g_race_laps_limit defaults to 7 in the port; Base (-1) consults the mapinfo laplimit.; Qualifying timelimit and the qualifying-then-race limit-stashing/restore are unwired (SaveRaceLimits has no caller).

[logic] race.checkpoint.entities: Port assumes the finish is checkpoint index 0; Base's race_timed_checkpoint can be the highest CP (spawnflags & 8) on tracks where start != finish.; Defrag target_checkpoint / target_startTimer / target_stopTimer chain not modeled for Race (CTS-only no-ops).;

[logic] race.checkpoint.ordered_crossing: The spawnflag-4 wrong-order kill (CheckpointTouch -> Combat.Damage 10000, Race.cs:328-329) is DEAD: no caller passes punishWrongWay:true (WireObjectiveSpawns:1947 spawns every checkpoint with the flag off), so a wrong-order crossing is still merely ignored on
[timing] race.lap.timing_scoring: Lap time uses wall Api.Clock.Time, not the QC race_movetime per-frame fractional accumulator; lap times drift from Base under frame-time jitter or pause.

[logic] race.penalty.zones: Freeze release restores MoveType.Walk unconditionally (Base just clears the freeze; a swimming/flying racer would be forced to Walk).; disableclientprediction = 2 (Base) is not set during the freeze; the client may mispredict through the freeze.; RACE_NET_PENA

[logic/values/timing] race.qualifying.mode: Retract uses Clients.Spawn (a normal spawn), not the Base checkpoint/grid-aware respawn (race_RetractPlayer + spawn_evalfunc by race_place).; independent_players / MAKE_INDEPENDENT_PLAYER (solo scoring isolation) not modeled.; Qualifying checkpoint records + R

[values/timing] race.qualifying.transition: Wave-5 wired SaveRaceLimits (at Race activation when g_race_qualifying==2) + a live caller for TransitionQualifyingToRace (GameWorld.RunCheckRulesWorld: the elapsed qualifying timelimit converts the session to a real race instead of ending). The qualifying-tim

[logic/values] race.spawn.grid_respawn: race_place START-GRID ordering still missing: racers do not get ordered start positions in a multi-racer race (no race_place read / info_player_race grid).; trigger_race_checkpoint_verify map-load validation (missing-spawn error) not performed.

[logic/values/timing] race.movement.quantization: Analog (joystick) move input is not snapped to keyboard octants -> an analog precision advantage Base removes for fairness.; race_movetime per-frame fractional accumulator absent (lap timing uses wall clock instead).; cl_movecliptokeyboard 2 is not stuffed to
[logic/values] race.bot.role: Bots have no race role; they do not navigate toward the next checkpoint, so a bot-filled race has idle/wandering bots.

[logic/values] race.hud.mod_icon_rankings: The HUD_Mod_Race MOD-ICON (the floating personal-best + server-best icon + the new-record/new-rank/fail medal flash with rank ordinal, faded over 5 s) is still NOT ported — ModIconsPanel has no Race case. (Note: the split-timer panel #8 has its OWN internal me

[logic/values/timing] race.speed_awards: race_SpeedAwardFrame (per-frame top-speed tracking, round + all-time speed awards broadcast to clients, RaceTimerPanel speed-at-CP readout baseline) is not ported. RaceTimerPanel has RaceCheckpointSpeed/BestSpeed fields but they are never fed.

[values] cts.mode.register_latch: MUTATOR_ONADD does not force g_race_qualifying=1 / independent_players=1; Qualifying merely reads the cvar (default 1) so the common path matches, but a server setting g_race_qualifying 0 diverges from QC's forced-on behavior.; gametype_init defaults (timelimi

[timing] cts.timing.start_finish: Run time is still wall-clock (Api.Clock.Time, Cts.cs:245/259) not the QC frame-accumulated .race_movetime; at variable frame rate / with prediction the recorded time can drift from QC's deterministic value.

[logic/values/timing] cts.checkpoints.intermediate: target_checkpoint and trigger_race_checkpoint are consumed as no-ops for CTS (GameWorld:2093-2094 documents them as Race-only); defrag-style maps lose ordered checkpoint enforcement.; No 'went backwards' detection, no spawnflag-4 backward-damage, no per-checkp

[logic/values/timing] cts.physics.force_keyboard: Analog movement is not snapped to keyboard cardinals/45-degree diagonals, so analog-stick players keep an unfair record advantage.; The per-frame .race_movetime accumulator (frame-rate-independent run clock) is not modeled; run timing uses wall-clock.; cl_move

[logic] cts.weapon.shotgun_only: DIVERGENCE: Base WantWeapon only governs the START loadout (want_weapon -> start_items); CTS players CAN still pick up map weapons mid-run. The port re-clears OwnedWeaponSet to shotgun-only EVERY prethink frame (OnPlayerPreThink -> ForceShotgunOnly, Cts.cs:484

[logic] cts.items.loot_filter: The port has no item-class registry exposing ITEM_IS_LOOT / .monster_item, so OnFilterItemDefinition approximates the Base test by classname tag (id.StartsWith("dropped") / contains "monster", Cts.cs:461-467). Items whose classname does not carry the 'dropped'
[logic/values/timing] cts.speedaward: No per-round best-speed tracking/broadcast and no persisted all-time best speed; the HUD speed-award display has no data.

[logic] cts.map_reset: No reset_map_global handler for CTS: the in-memory per-checkpoint records are not cleared on map reset, places are not event-logged, and the qualifying==2->race collapse is not handled (CTS pins qualifying=1 so the collapse is mostly moot). Wave-5 added _check

[logic/values] cts.lifecycle.prepare_player: Cts registers a PlayerSpawn hook, but it ONLY forces the Shotgun loadout (Cts.cs:477-481) — it does NOT do the race_PreparePlayer bookkeeping (checkpoint reset to -1, race_SendAll, respawn-checkpoint/spotref recording, the spawn==qualifying re-prepare-vs-retra

[logic/values] cts.bot.role: No CTS bot role: bots are not routed to their next-expected race-checkpoint waypoint (with the raw-touch redirect for unreachable checkpoints), so bots do not actually run the CTS course.

[logic] cts.records.getrecords: No GetRecords/getrankings console reply: CommandReplies.GetRankings still returns the honest-empty 'No records are available for the map: <map>' (CommandReplies.cs:143-147) rather than reading the RaceRecords CTS table. The data exists (the scoreboard already

[values] freezetag.mode.registration: Match limit semantics differ: Base reads mapinfo pointlimit (10) / leadlimit (6) via g_freezetag_point_limit=-1 passthrough; port keys off fraglimit/leadlimit. Live default round target (10) coincidentally matches, leadlimit 6 likely not honored.

[values] freezetag.unfreeze: Does not set pauseregen_finished after revive (minor regen-timing divergence) — still open.; Does not delete an ice entity (not spawned in the first place); the waypoint sprite IS now cleared (it stops being collected once IsFrozen flips false).
[values] freezetag.revive.score: No nade revival bonus (g_nades_bonus_score_low) on a completed revive — minor, requires the nades mutator.

[logic/values/timing] freezetag.revive.auto_reducible: Shooting a frozen enemy still does nothing to their thaw timer; the core 'avoid damaging frozen enemies' tactic is absent. No FT DamageCalculate handler exists, and no revive_auto_reducible reference in src/game.

[logic/values] freezetag.damage.frozen_invuln: Frozen players (HP=1) are STILL NOT made damage-immune; chip damage re-kills the frozen body. DamageSystem only special-cases frozen for teamplay-nullify bypass (IsFrozenStat ~177) and pain-sound suppression (~471) — the subtract path (~466) applies health dam

[values] freezetag.win.roundlimit: Lead-limit default (Base 6 via mapinfo) likely not honored (port reads leadlimit cvar, default 0 = off).

[logic/values] freezetag.presentation.ice_model: No distinct ice block entity (random 1-of-20 frame, team-colored, following the player at origin-16) — frozen players only get an icy-blue colormod on their normal model.

[logic/values/timing] freezetag.bots: Bots have no FT-specific freeing/offense behavior; they won't deliberately revive frozen teammates, and frozen-target attack gating (Bot_ForbidAttack) is not modeled in FT.

[logic] invasion.win.hunt: Base derives the HUNT win from the live g_monsters list (no non-respawned monster remaining); the port checks its own Wave.MaxSpawned/Killed counters + LiveMonsters (Invasion.cs:460-465), so map-placed (pre-existing) monsters are not the win source.

[logic/values] invasion.win.stage: STAGE ends on the FIRST player touch; Base requires ceil(realplayers * min(1,count)) distinct REAL players (default count=0.7 → 70%, bots excluded) to have set inv_endreached before winning. TriggerRoundEnd sets RoundEndReached unconditionally and the touch vo

[logic] invasion.win.point_limit: Base's limit is a per-score-row total via GameRules_limit_score (the leading player's KILLS); the port latches MatchEnded on the cumulative MonstersKilled total (sum across ALL players), which approximates but is not the same as Base's leading-player limit.

[logic] invasion.client.hide_item_stats: The scoreboard item-stats (weapon accuracy) panel is not suppressed in Invasion; Base hides it because monsters aren't valid accuracy targets.
